definition:
Providing an LLM with a small number
                        of examples that demonstrate the task and desired output before asking it to
                        perform a similar task. This technique is an application of in-context
                        learning, where models learn from examples

Few-Shot Prompting is a glossary term used to describe an important concept, capability, or operational pattern within artificial intelligence, digital systems, and modern enterprise technology. At a high level, it refers to how organizations structure knowledge, processes, models, interactions, or
Prompt engineering technique: Few-Shot Prompting. A method for structuring prompts to improve AI model outputs.

虽然大型语言模型展示了惊人的零样本能力，但在使用零样本设置时，它们在更复杂的任务上仍然表现不佳。少样本提示可以作为一种技术，以启用上下文学习，我们在提示中提供演示以引导模型实现更好的性能。演示作为后续示例的条件，我们希望模型生成响应。 根据 [Touvron et al. 2023](https://arxiv.org/pdf/2302.13971.pdf) 等人的在 2023 年的论文，当模型规模足够大时，小样本提示特性开始出现 [(Kaplan et al., 2020)](https://arxiv.org/abs/2001.08361)。 让我们通过[Brown等人2020年](htt

A Python-first configuration library that sets the
values of functions and classes without invasive code or infrastructure.
In the case of Pax—and other ML codebases—these functions and
classes represent models and training
hyperparameters.

A database migration method that uses continuous data replication through
                            change data capture to migrate data
                        in the shortest time possible, instead of using a phased approach. The
                        objective is to keep downtime to a minimum.

A family of relatively small Gemini models optimized for speed
and low latency. Flash models are designed for a wide
range of applications where quick responses and high throughput are crucial.

A high-performance open-source
 library for
deep learning built on top of JAX. Flax provides functions
for training neural networks, as well
as methods for evaluating their performance.

An open-source Transformer
library,
built on Flax, designed primarily for natural language processing
and multimodal research.

The portion of a Long Short-Term Memory
cell that regulates the flow of information through the cell.
Forget gates maintain context by deciding which information to discard
from the cell state.

A very large pre-trained model trained on an enormous
and diverse training set. A foundation model can do both

A metric for evaluating an ML model's generated text.
The fraction of successes is the number of "successful" generated text
outputs divided by the total number of generated text outputs. For example,
if a large language model generated 10 blocks
of code, five of which were successful,

A function that takes a function as input and returns a transformed function
as output. JAX uses function transformations.

Abbreviation for generative adversarial
network.

The ecosystem comprising Google's most advanced AI. Elements of this ecosystem
include:

Google's state-of-the-art Transformer-based
multimodal models. Gemini models are specifically
designed to integrate with agents.

A family of lightweight open models built from the same
research and technology used to create the Gemini models. Several
different Gemma models are available, each providing different features, such as
vision, code, and instruction following. See Gemma
for details.
Abbreviation for generative AI.

A model's ability to make correct predictions on new,
previously unseen data. A model that can generalize is the opposite
of a model that is overfitting.

A plot of both training loss and
validation loss as a function of the number of
iterations.

A generalization of least squares regression
models, which are based on
Gaussian
noise, to other
types of models based on other types of noise, such as
Poisson noise
or
categorical noise. Examples of generalized linear models include:

language models, some metrics compare generated text against
reference text. For example, suppose you are
trying to determine how effectively an ML model translates from French

A system to create new data in which a generator creates
data and a discriminator determines whether that
created data is valid or invalid.

A subset of AI models that have been
                        trained on large amounts of data and that can use a simple text prompt to
                        create new content and artifacts, such as images, videos, text, and audio.

An approach in which lower and upper environments use different branches
                        in a source code repository. The Gitflow workflow is considered legacy, and
                        the trunk-based
                            workflow is the modern, preferred approach.

A set of manually curated data that captures ground truth.
Teams can use one or more golden datasets to evaluate a model's quality.
